summary: Archive 50 invalid benchmark runs, retain 7 valid runs based on 3 validity criteria
context: With 55 benchmark runs accumulated, needed to determine which data is valid for the paper. Three independent validity requirements identified: (1) plugin isolation via --setting-sources '' (commit bddefdf+), (2) standardized condition implementations with BASE_CLAUDE_MD (commit d7420c1+), (3) twining-lite tool restriction enforcement (commit 0ff8b25+).
rationale: Mapped every run's harness commit SHA against the 3 validity boundaries. Checked pre-fix runs for contamination (run 9be7a749 had 168+215 Twining calls in baseline/shared-markdown — confirmed contamination). Rescued 2 runs: 5ab87a48 (post all fixes, interrupted but 2 scenarios complete) and 16a2e4e1 (pre-bddefdf but 0 Twining contamination in baseline/shared-markdown). Removed invalid twining-lite data from 16a2e4e1 and incomplete architecture-cascade from 5ab87a48.

alternatives.option: Keep all runs and note caveats in the paper
alternatives.reason_rejected: Pre-d7420c1 runs used different CLAUDE.md content per condition — fundamentally incomparable, not just noisy
Would lose 16a2e4e1 baseline/shared-markdown data that is demonstrably clean (0 Twining calls) and adds n=3 to sprint-sim

assumptions: Pre-bddefdf runs with 0 Twining tool calls in baseline/shared-markdown are functionally equivalent to post-bddefdf runs for those conditions
Rescored data from old runs is valid when using current scorer code on original raw transcripts
